summary: A public runx skill and upstream pull request were published for secret-catcher, giving runx a concrete security-review example that future contributors can inspect, run, and discuss.

observations.summary: The runx registry page explains the secret-catcher skill, shows its green harness result, and provides the runx install/run command.
observations.audience: runx users and agent-tooling contributors evaluating practical governed skill examples
observations.why_allowed: The artifact is published in the runx registry itself and is a first-party place for project examples, not an unrelated promotion venue.

observations.summary: The upstream PR adds the secret-catcher skill package to the runx project for maintainer review.
observations.why_allowed: A pull request to the target open-source project is an expected venue for useful project contributions.

observations.summary: The public fork exposes SKILL.md, X.yaml, and run.mjs so reviewers can inspect the package shape and implementation.
observations.audience: maintainers, reviewers, and future skill authors
observations.why_allowed: A public fork is normal source provenance for an upstream pull request.

observations.summary: The skill demonstrates a practical pre-merge secret scan that reports hashes and redaction proposals without echoing credential values.
observations.audience: developers who need safe evidence for code review or receipt packets
observations.why_allowed: The support action is a technical example with reusable value, not a star-only or reciprocal-promotion claim.

reviewer_notes: The public support action is the combination of a live runx registry skill and an upstream PR to runxhq/runx.
The artifact links directly to runx.ai and to the canonical runxhq/runx repository.
The work is specific to runx governed skills and avoids screenshots, star proof, star trading, or generic promotion.
